title: agrostis alba subsp. castellana
intro: Agrostis alba subsp. castellana, also known as Castilian bent grass, is a subspecies of grass belonging to the Poaceae family. It is native to the Iberian Peninsula and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including wound healing, reducing fever, and alleviating digestive issues. Traditionally, herbal preparations of Agrostis alba subsp. castellana include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
